title: Hooks, Quality Gates, Signing, and Repository Security · History Brief: Hook Lifecycle
summary: Add useful local feedback without pretending client-side hooks enforce server policy or executing untrusted repository code silently. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git hook lifecycle increment in a bounded Git automation and trust policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Map client and server hook names to operations, inputs, environment, bypass behavior, and failure effects.
Compare old and rewritten ranges by patch, tree, tests, metadata, and range-diff before replacing a shared reference.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Hook Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
theory case — A cloned repository proposes a pre-commit hook, diff driver, and build script. Determine what Git runs automatically, what is bypassable, and which server or CI gate owns enforcement before trust.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Hook Lifecycle contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for map client and server hook names to operations, inputs, environment, bypass behavior, and failure effects.

content.text: A cloned repository proposes a pre-commit hook, diff driver, and build script. Determine what Git runs automatically, what is bypassable, and which server or CI gate owns enforcement before trust.

content.text: Map client and server hook names to operations, inputs, environment, bypass behavior, and failure effects. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Hook Lifecycle while producing a bounded Git automation and trust policy.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A cloned repository proposes a pre-commit hook, diff driver, and build script. Determine what Git runs automatically, what is bypassable, and which server or CI gate owns enforcement before trust. Competency probe: Map client and server hook names to operations, inputs, environment, bypass behavior, and failure effects. A defensible solution applies Hook Lifecycle, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a bounded Git automation and trust policy.

content.code:
# Worked focus: git-hook-lifecycle — Map client and server hook names to operations, inputs, environment, bypass behavior, and failure effects.
# Evidence: changed Git case 4
# hypothesis: the mismatch is isolated to one repository layer
git status --short --branch
git diff
git diff --staged
git log --graph --decorate --oneline -n 6
# observation: name worktree, index, ref, and graph evidence separately
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Cloning a repository installs and runs all versioned hooks automatically. This fails the Hook Lifecycle contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
